Originally formulated as a means of dissolving everlasting adhesive, this powerful solvent can break almost any adhesive's grip. markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 295 **Price** 55 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**1 minute (Interact) --- This peculiar amber adhesive bonds two surfaces together almost inseparably. A single flask covers an area up to 1 square foot and must be used all at once to form a single bond between two surfaces. If the activation is interrupted, the bond fails, and the adhesive is wasted.
Once two surfaces are joined with everlasting adhesive, a creature can separate them only with a successful DC 50 Athletics check. The adhered objects tend to break before the adhesive does unless they're particularly durable, though a creature determined to separate the objects can break off the parts connected by the everlasting adhesive and later [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) the objects.
The adhesive can affect creatures only if they're willing, and its bond can be broken by exfoliating the outermost layer of skin.
name: Everlasting Adhesive

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 295 **Price** 3 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Activating this vial of colorless liquid requires inserting a small amount of another chemical or material, such as blood or mud. The vial reacts rapidly, transforming into a murky, reddish-brown substance for a brief moment before turning clear once more.
Once activated, the dye remains potent for up to 10 minutes, during which time you can spend 1 minute to brush it onto a single object of up to 1 Bulk or across the ground in a single 5-foot square. Where the dye comes in contact with an exact match for the activating chemical, it takes on a bright blue hue while staying transparent in areas with no activating component present.
name: Forensic Dye

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 295 **Price** 3 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- This pale-blue ink dries rapidly, becoming fully transparent 1 minute after application. The ink glows red when exposed to heat, such as that from a torch or other open flame. This glow lasts only as long as the ink is exposed to heat, after which the ink becomes invisible again. The crafter of the ghost ink can alter the formula slightly to instead make the ink sensitive to sunlight, starlight, magical light, or heatless light created by an alchemical effect, such as a glow rod.
While the text isn't glowing, a creature closely examining a surface marked with ghost ink can detect the presence of the ink with a successful DC 25 Perception check. On a critical success, they can make out the ink well enough to use Society to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363). One vial of ghost ink is sufficient to write a page worth of text.
name: Ghost Ink

[Matchstick](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3356)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 295, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 251

**Price** 2 sp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

---

An alchemical substance applied to one end of this tiny wooden stick ignites when struck against a rough surface. Creating a flame with a matchstick is much faster than creating a flame with flint and steel. You can ignite it and touch it to a flammable object as part of the same Interact action. A matchstick remains lit for 1 round, after which it's consumed and extinguished.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Matchstick

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 295, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 554 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 2 **Activate**([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) or 1 or more days --- An [alchemist](/Classes.aspx?ID=56) with the [Craft Philosopher's Stone](/Feats.aspx?ID=127) feat adds the formula for this item to their [formula book](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2724). This allows them to create a philosopher's stone once per month during their daily preparations using advanced alchemy. Unlike other items created with advanced alchemy, the philosopher's stone remains potent for 1 month or until the alchemist creates a new one. This is the only way to create a philosopher's stone. At a glance, a philosopher's stone appears to be an ordinary, sooty piece of natural rock. Breaking the rock open with a [Force Open](/Actions.aspx?ID=2375) action (DC 35) reveals a cavity at the stone's heart. The cavity is lined with a rare type of quicksilver that can transmute base metals into precious metals or create an elixir of rejuvenation. To use the quicksilver, you must be legendary in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) and have the [Alchemical Crafting](/Feats.aspx?ID=5117) feat. You can then use the stone's quicksilver for one of two effects.
  • You can apply the stone's quicksilver to an [infused](/Traits.aspx?ID=797) [true elixir of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3308) using an Interact action. This turns the elixir into an infused [elixir of rejuvenation](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3309) instantaneously. This doesn't require any crafting time or additional materials.
  • You can spend up to a month of [downtime](/Rules.aspx?ID=2008) applying the quicksilver either to iron to create silver or to lead to create gold. Treat this as a 20th-level task to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364) using Crafting, except that you create 500 gp worth of your chosen metal per day on a success or 750 gp worth per day on a critical success.
name: Philosopher's Stone

This sticky salve stubbornly holds wounds closed and encourages swift natural healing. You can activate the salve in either of the following ways.

**Activate—Administer Stitch** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=2396) without requiring healer's tools. You can either gain a +2 item bonus to the Medicine check or use the salve's Medicine modifier of +13 instead of your own.

**Activate—Stitch Wounds** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You gain a +2 item bonus to the triggering Medicine check. If you roll a success on the Medicine check, you get a critical success instead.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 296, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 251

**Price** 2 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

---

You can apply snake oil on a wound or other outward symptom of an affliction or condition (such as sores from a disease or discoloration from a poison). For the next hour, the symptom disappears, and the [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) or afflicted creature doesn't feel as if it still has the wound or affliction, though all effects remain. A creature can uncover the ruse by succeeding at a DC 17 Perception check, but only if it uses a [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to specifically examine the snake oil's effects.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Snake Oil

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Timeless Salts](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3362)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=528" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 296

**Price** 14 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

You sprinkle these salts onto a single object up to 10 cubic feet in volume and no more than 40 Bulk to preserve it for 1 week. The object doesn't decay, and effects that require the object to be fresh don't count the time passing during this duration as having elapsed. When sprinkled on a corpse, this extends the period in which a creature can be revived by magic as well as the wait time required before a corpse can be targeted again with talking corpse. The salts prevent ordinary pests from consuming the target (such as maggots for a corpse or moths for a piece of clothing). Any creature can use an Interact action to disperse the salts from an unattended object and end this effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Timeless Salts

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 297 **Price** 40 gp --- You create a snare that causes three 3rd-level moderate alchemical bombs of the same type to explode when a creature triggers the snare. The target and all creatures in adjacent squares must attempt a DC 24 Reflex save, as the snare deals damage equal to three times the direct hit damage from one of the component bombs (for example, 6d6 electricity damage from three moderate bottled lightnings) with no splash damage or other effects. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and no other effects.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage. It also takes all other effects of a direct hit from one of the component bombs (such as [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) from bottled lightning [or persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) from an acid flask).
**Critical Failure** The creature takes double damage, plus all other effects of a direct hit (as failure). **Craft Requirements** Supply three of the same damaging 3rd-level moderate alchemical bomb.

When a creature enters the square, the magnets and ropes deploy, weighing down the creature's weapons and limbs. The creature must attempt a DC 18 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes a -1 status penalty to attack rolls for 1 round or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 18).
**Failure** The creature takes a –2 status penalty to attack rolls for 1 minute or until it Escapes (DC 18).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature drops any metallic items it's holding.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 298 **Price** 900 gp --- You arrange a spiky cage of bones, particularly tough vegetation, or other material to spring up when disturbed. The snare deals 10d8 piercing damage to the first creature to enter this square; that creature must attempt a DC 34 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is knocked aside by the snare's deployment, takes half damage, and is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the end of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature is captured by the cage, taking full damage and falling [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88). It's [immobilized](/Conditions.aspx?ID=81) while it remains within the cage. It can get free by [Escaping](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 34) or by destroying the cage (AC 30, Fort +18, Ref +24, Hardness 5, HP 30, object immunities).
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes double damage.

When a creature enters the square, a deadly flying wheel of spinning blades launches at it, making a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with an attack modifier of +35 that deals 8d8 slashing damage on a hit. Once on each of your turns, you can use an Interact action within 120 feet of the wheel to cause it to [Fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) up to 60 feet toward the creature it's chasing and make another Strike if it's within 5 feet of its target after it moves. After 1 minute, the spinning ceases, and the wheel falls to the ground. Creatures can destroy the wheel to stop it (AC 37, Fort +29, Ref +20, Hardness 10, HP 200, object immunities).

</column>
name: Flying Blade Wheel Snare

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 299 **Price** 3 gp --- This snare is often used to mark intruders for later tracking or identification. When you create this snare, you must decide whether to make it a dye or a scent marker. Either type of marking grants a +2 circumstance bonus to [Track](/Actions.aspx?ID=2408) the creature for up to 24 hours or until the dye or scent is washed off (requiring at least a gallon of water and 10 minutes of scrubbing). A creature that enters a square of the snare must attempt a DC 17 Reflex save. **Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Failure** The snare marks the creature.
**Critical Failure** The snare marks the creature, and the creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn.
name: Marking Snare

---

A subtle snare used in hunting or tracking, a signaling snare often consists of carefully prepared earth, piled sand or stones, specific arrangements of vegetation, and so forth. When a creature enters a square of a signaling snare, nothing happens to the creature, but instead it causes a small, unobtrusive disruption to the terrain that allows the snare's creator or another creature who knows what to look for to determine whether a creature of the appropriate size entered the square.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Signaling Snare

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 300 **Price** 15 gp --- This snare explodes in a burst of cloying powder that can cling to a creature stepping into its square. A creature that enters the square of a stalker bane snare must attempt a DC 20 Reflex save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** Powder sticks to the target, causing it to leave behind telltale footprints. Being [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) makes the target [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79), rather than [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96), to creatures that could see it if it weren't invisible.
**Failure** Powder clumps on the target, constantly flaking away. Being invisible makes the target concealed, rather than hidden or undetected, to creatures that could see it if it weren't invisible.
**Critical Failure** As failure, and the creature is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn.
name: Stalker Bane Snare

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 300 **Price** 15 gp --- You set a cunning wire to trip a creature. A Medium or smaller creature that enters this snare's square must attempt a DC 21 Reflex save.
If you want to create a trip snare to trip a larger creature, you must create a group of contiguous snares of a length equal to the edge of that larger creature's space, and the creature must be moving such that it moves into the full set of snares. For example, three trip snares in a 15-foot-line can trip a Huge creature coming down a corridor into the line of snares. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of its next turn.
**Failure** The creature falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).
**Critical Failure** The creature falls prone and takes 1d6 bludgeoning damage.

The creation of these arrows was inspired by an encounter with a horned archon scout who sought to peacefully restrain an escaping foe. When an activated _antler arrow_ hits a target, glowing antlers extend to pin it down. The target must succeed at a DC 16 Reflex save or become stuck to the surface, taking the [critical specialization effects of a bow](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=3). 

If the hit with the _antler arrow_ is a critical hit and you have access to the bow critical specialization effect, the DC of the Athletics check increases to 15.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 301 **Price** 130 gp **Ammunition** arrow **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- The head of this arrow is made from gleaming copper. When an activated storm arrow hits a target, it's buffeted by raging winds and struck by a bolt of lightning that deals 3d12 electricity damage, and the target must attempt a DC 25 Reflex saving throw. If this arrow is shot from a weapon with a shock property rune, the save DC increases to 27, though the attack doesn't benefit from the _shock property_ rune itself. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature takes half damage and isn't affected by the wind.
**Failure** The creature takes full damage and is buffeted by winds for 1 round, taking a –2 circumstance penalty to ranged attack rolls and a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its fly Speed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature takes double damage.
name: Storm Arrow

When activated _terrifying ammunition_ damages a creature, the creature's mind is filled with visions of its failures, its tragedies, and eventually, its own death. The creature must attempt a DC 20 Will save. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature is [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1.
**Failure** The creature is frightened 1 and can't reduce its frightened value below 1 until it spends an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561) trait, to calm itself down.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but the creature is frightened 2.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 302 **Price** 17 gp **Ammunition** arrow **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- The shaft of this arrow is covered in fine green scales, and its iron head comes to a pair of points almost like fangs. After an activated viper arrow hits a target, the arrow transforms into a [viper](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3200). The target is affected by the viper's venom, as if it had been bitten. The viper then lands in an open space adjacent to the target.
The viper has the [summoned](/Traits.aspx?ID=706) trait and acts at the end of your turn, even though you didn't use the [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) action. It's under the GM's control, but it generally attacks the creature the arrow struck. The viper vanishes after 1 minute or when slain. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694).
name: Viper Arrow

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 303 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- This fine golden oil comes in a small blue canister. Carefully spreading the oil over an object turns it into an [animated object](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=360) of the same type. For example, sprinkling it on a statue makes an animated statue. If the animated object's level would be greater than 3, the oil struggles to animate it and ultimately fails.
This animated object has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait, meaning it can use 2 actions when you Command it. You can issue a Command for the current turn as part of the activation. The object remains animated for 1 minute before falling inert. As usual, you can have a maximum of four minions under your control. Oil of Dynamism **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 303 **Price** 85 gp Oil of Dynamism (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 303 **Price** 1,400 gp --- You can animate an object with a level of 11 or lower.
name: Oil of Dynamism

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 303 **Price** 25 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- This bright oil, first created by humans as a tool to help them fight in darkness, holds flecks of tiny gemstones in suspension and smells like a struck matchstick.
The first time a weapon coated with this oil damages a creature, the wound glows with light for 1 minute. If the creature is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), the light's position means it's merely [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) to creatures that would otherwise be unable to see it, rather than [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96). The light also negates the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition due to lighting conditions. If the coated weapon doesn't damage a creature within 1 hour, the oil sloughs off and loses its power.
name: Oil of Revelation

This shimmering paste has many properties of a [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671) spell. When you slather it onto a creature or object, the target gains a +1 circumstance bonus to AC for 1 round. The first time a physical attack or a [_force barrage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536) hits the target during that round, the oil prevents 5 damage from that attack or spell, and then the oil's effect ends.

</column>
name: Shielding Salve

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Upon imbibing this potion, you take on the appearance of a specific type of creature for 2d12 hours. The type of creature is determined when the potion is created. For example, you might have a _potion of elf disguise_ or _potion of frog disguise_. Drinking the potion doesn't impart the knowledge of how long the effect lasts; the GM rolls the duration in secret.
The disguise doesn't change your traits or statistics, nor does it give you any of the special abilities of the creature you're imitating. It might affect what items you can hold or wear (for example, your new form might lack opposable thumbs). The potion shrinks you down to a minimum of size Small or maintains your size if the creature is your size or larger. For example, if you are Medium and drink a potion of fire giant disguise, you'll take on the appearance of a fire giant but remain Medium. The creature has to be of a specific kind, such as “leopard” or “lion” rather than just “cat,” or “fire giant” or “ogre” rather than just “giant,” but the potion can't cause you to mimic a specific individual creature.
The effects of this potion use the same rules as the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) activity of Deception. Onlookers always assume you're the chosen type of creature unless they're actively Seeking. You gain a +4 status bonus to your Deception DC against such Perception checks and add your level even if untrained. Potion of Disguise (Lesser) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 258 **Price** 30 gp **Bulk** L **Craft Requirements** Initial raw materials must include a bit of blood, hair, or flesh of the creature the potion will allow the drinker to imitate. Potion of Disguise (Moderate) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 258 **Price** 100 gp **Bulk** L --- The _moderate potion of disguise_ can increase your size if the creature is larger than you, to a maximum of Large, and it can shrink you to a creature of size Tiny. This doesn't change any of your statistics, with the exception of reducing your reach to 0 feet as a Tiny creature. **Craft Requirements** Initial raw materials must include a bit of blood, hair, or flesh of the creature the potion will allow the drinker to imitate. Potion of Disguise (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 258 **Price** 300 gp **Bulk** L --- While drinking a _greater potion of disguise_, if you picture the specific form you want to transform into, the potion will change you into that form. You can attempt to Impersonate a specific individual, though you still need to roll Deception.
name: Potion of Disguise

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- Upon imbibing this potion, you take on the appearance of a specific type of creature for 2d12 hours. The type of creature is determined when the potion is created. For example, you might have a _potion of elf disguise_ or _potion of frog disguise_. Drinking the potion doesn't impart the knowledge of how long the effect lasts; the GM rolls the duration in secret.
The disguise doesn't change your traits or statistics, nor does it give you any of the special abilities of the creature you're imitating. It might affect what items you can hold or wear (for example, your new form might lack opposable thumbs). The potion shrinks you down to a minimum of size Small or maintains your size if the creature is your size or larger. For example, if you are Medium and drink a potion of fire giant disguise, you'll take on the appearance of a fire giant but remain Medium. The creature has to be of a specific kind, such as “leopard” or “lion” rather than just “cat,” or “fire giant” or “ogre” rather than just “giant,” but the potion can't cause you to mimic a specific individual creature.
The effects of this potion use the same rules as the [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2388) activity of Deception. Onlookers always assume you're the chosen type of creature unless they're actively Seeking. You gain a +4 status bonus to your Deception DC against such Perception checks and add your level even if untrained. Potion of Disguise (Lesser) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 258 **Price** 30 gp **Bulk** L **Craft Requirements** Initial raw materials must include a bit of blood, hair, or flesh of the creature the potion will allow the drinker to imitate. Potion of Disguise (Moderate) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 258 **Price** 100 gp **Bulk** L --- The _moderate potion of disguise_ can increase your size if the creature is larger than you, to a maximum of Large, and it can shrink you to a creature of size Tiny. This doesn't change any of your statistics, with the exception of reducing your reach to 0 feet as a Tiny creature. **Craft Requirements** Initial raw materials must include a bit of blood, hair, or flesh of the creature the potion will allow the drinker to imitate. Potion of Disguise (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 258 **Price** 300 gp **Bulk** L --- While drinking a _greater potion of disguise_, if you picture the specific form you want to transform into, the potion will change you into that form. You can attempt to Impersonate a specific individual, though you still need to roll Deception.

    level="1"
    right="Item 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Potion of Emergency Escape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3403)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=672" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [GM Core](/Sources.aspx?ID=218) pg. 259

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The stopper for a potion of emergency escape is crafted to easily snap open in dire circumstances. When you drink this potion, for 1 minute you become [fleeing](/Conditions.aspx?ID=74) from all hostile creatures you’re aware of. You gain a +40-foot status bonus to all Speeds as long as this fleeing condition lasts. You immediately [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Potion of Emergency Escape

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />

<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A potion of retaliation is available in four varieties—acid, cold, electricity, and fire—each with a faint shimmer of the energy it contains. For 1 minute after drinking a _potion of retaliation_, you glow with a faint aura of that energy, and a creature that touches you (such as by making an unarmed attack or using a spell with a range of touch against you) takes damage of that type. The moderate, greater, and major versions also damage an adjacent creature that hits you with a melee weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).

A potion of retaliation is available in four varieties—acid, cold, electricity, and fire—each with a faint shimmer of the energy it contains. For 1 minute after drinking a _potion of retaliation_, you glow with a faint aura of that energy, and a creature that touches you (such as by making an unarmed attack or using a spell with a range of touch against you) takes damage of that type. The moderate, greater, and major versions also damage an adjacent creature that hits you with a melee weapon [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306).

>
[Ration Tonic](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3405)
</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Potion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=672" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This slender vial appears to hold clean, clear water with a faintly fruity scent. Drinking a _ration tonic_ magically nourishes you with the equivalent of a day's worth of food and water. The tonic has a subtle, pleasant taste, its particulars chosen when the potion is crafted.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 1"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ration Tonic
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 259

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ration Tonic (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 304, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 259

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

A _greater ration tonic_ nourishes you for 1 week instead of 1 day, distributing the nourishment in a constant and healthy flow across the week.
name: Ration Tonic

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Balisse Feather](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3406)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 305

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You deal damage using the affixed weapon to a creature that has the [unholy trait](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) or that you witnessed harm an ally, an innocent, or noncombatant within the last minute.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, fire-red feather smolders from the weapon it adorns. When you activate the feather, the creature you damaged burns with sacred light. The creature must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or take a –2 status penalty to AC and saving throws and reduce its resistances by 5. These effects last until the end of your next turn. This item has no effect on a creature with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 12"
pfs="Standard"
>
Balisse Feather
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 305

**Price** 400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 16"
pfs="Standard"
>
Balisse Feather (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 305

**Price** 2,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The DC is 35, and the creature’s resistances are reduced by 10.
name: Balisse Feather

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Holy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=517" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 305

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** You deal damage using the affixed weapon to a creature that has the [unholy trait](/Traits.aspx?ID=521) or that you witnessed harm an ally, an innocent, or noncombatant within the last minute.

 
</row>

</column>

---

This long, fire-red feather smolders from the weapon it adorns. When you activate the feather, the creature you damaged burns with sacred light. The creature must succeed at a DC 29 Will save or take a –2 status penalty to AC and saving throws and reduce its resistances by 5. These effects last until the end of your next turn. This item has no effect on a creature with the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait.

When you activate the navette, you gain the benefit of the fighter's [Determination](/Feats.aspx?ID=4841) class feat, with a counteract rank of 8 and a counteract modifier of +22.
If you have the Determination feat, you can use your own modifier if it's better.

[Hunter's Bane](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3408)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Detection" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=574" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Talisman" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=709" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 305

**Price** 6 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** affixed to armor

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Free Action" /><row>([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))</row></row>

 

**Trigger** A [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) enemy hits you with an attack

**Requirement** You're trained in Survivial.
</row>

</column>

---

This talisman is a ring of dried, interwoven pieces of straw. When you activate the _hunter's bane_, you sense the exact location of the attacker. It becomes [observed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=84) by you if it was [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) from you or becomes hidden from you if it was [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96). If the attacker is behind lead, the _hunter's bane_ has no effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hunter's Bane

[Candle of Revealing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3412)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 306

</row>

</column>

---

When lit, this black candle's eerie blue flame reveals the presence of invisible creatures. Within a 10-foot radius of the lit candle, creatures don't benefit from the [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83) condition. Their bodies are outlined, not fully visible, so they're [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). Once lit, the candle burns for 1 minute, after which the effect ends. If extinguished, it can't be relit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Candle of Revealing

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 306 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- This black pouch contains what appears to be fine bone dust. Pouring the dust in a special pattern over a corpse turns it into an undead creature. The type of undead created depends on the condition of the corpse, resulting in either a [skeleton](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=472) or a [zombie](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=487). If the undead's level would be greater than 3, the dust fails to animate it. The body must be of an appropriate size and type for the undead you wish to create—for example, you must sprinkle the dust on a horse's skeleton to animate a skeletal horse. If more than one undead in the level range is appropriate, such as [skeletal guard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3193) or [skeletal champion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3194) for a Medium humanoid skeleton, you choose.
The animated undead has the [minion](/Traits.aspx?ID=653) trait, meaning it can use 2 actions when you Command it. You can issue a Command for the current turn as part of the activation. The undead creature remains animated for 1 minute before collapsing back into its corpse form. You can have a maximum of four minions under your control. Dust of Corpse Animation **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 306, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 257 **Price** 100 gp Dust of Corpse Animation (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 306, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 257 **Price** 1,500 gp --- You can animate an undead with a level of 11 or lower.
name: Dust of Corpse Animation

<traits>
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />

This black incense smells of fresh earth and ash. You activate the incense by lighting it, whereupon it fills a 10-foot emanation with oily smoke and potent void energy. Undead creatures gain fast healing 4 while in the area; though this healing comes from void energy, it doesn't negatively impact living creatures. Once lit, the incense burns for 1 minute, and it can't be extinguished.

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 306 **Price** 600 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Activate**([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104)) --- This thin golden candle bears the symbol of a specific deity emblazoned on its surface, surrounded by the iconography of that deity's faith. A _taper of sanctification_ must be dedicated to a deity who can be sanctified to [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) or [unholy](/Traits.aspx?ID=521), and has the corresponding trait. If the deity's sanctification lists both options, the crafter must choose one when the candle is made.
Once lit, this candle burns for 1 hour, and it can't be extinguished. When a willing creature spends the full hour within 10 feet of the lit candle engaging in prayer to the deity, that creature's [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) gain the holy or unholy trait (as appropriate for the candle) until the next time that creature makes their daily preparations. During this time, the creature is bound by the deity's anathema. If they violate anathema, they lose the benefit of the taper of sanctification. A holy creature can't benefit from an unholy taper of sanctification, nor can an unholy creature benefit from a holy one.
name: Taper of Sanctification

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 307 **Price** 75,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This small piece of embroidered cloth is inimical to all magic. **Activate—Nullify Magic** ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Effect** You cover a magic item with the cloth or wave the cloth near a magic effect and attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the effect or item. The cloth's counteract check modifier is +32, and its counteract rank is 10. Regardless of the result, the _cloth of nullification_ can't be activated again for 2d6 hours. On a success, the effect or item is deactivated for the same amount of time, and its duration, if any, continues to expire during that time. With a successful counteract check, you can instead choose to completely absorb the magic from the effect or item into the _cloth of nullification_. If you do, both become completely non-magical and their magic can't be recovered, even by the [_remake_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1649) spell.
The _cloth of nullification_ automatically fails to counteract most artifacts and similarly powerful items.
name: Cloth of Nullification

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Scrying" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=689" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 307

**Price** 575 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This fine wooden box is inlaid with Dwarven runes, with hinges and a clasp of iron. The box contains handfuls of fine sand.

**Activate—Replicate Earth** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You hold the box closed and, while envisioning the terrain, turn the box clockwise three times. When you open the box, the sand reveals, in miniature, the stone terrain surrounding you, to a range of 60 feet. This shows details of paths, hills, embankments, boulders, and even artificial structures like walls and ditches, as long as they're made of stone and earth. If you're underground, it reveals tunnels and voids in the earth within 60 feet at your current depth. The sand maintains its shape until you close the box.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Earthsight Box

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 307

**Price** 3 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This seemingly unremarkable, weathered silver coin bears the bust of an unnamed monarch on the face and a majestic bird on the tail. You can toss the coin without activating it, in which case it follows the normal laws of probability.

**Activate—Cheat Fate** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** You rub your thumb on one side of the coin with the intent of slightly tweaking the strands of fate, then flip the coin into the air in a coin toss. No matter how the toss is resolved—letting the coin fall to the ground, slapping it down on the back of your hand, or catching it on your open palm—it always lands with the side you rubbed face up.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Predictable Silver Piece

This ball of hempen twine resists efforts to unravel it by hand.

**Activate—Unravel Twine** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect**  You toss the ball of twine into a square within 20 feet. The twine then unravels and animates, attempting to Disarm or Trip (your choice) a creature in the square with a total of +9 to the Athletics check. At the end of your turn, the twine winds itself back into a ball and returns to your hand; if you don't have a free hand, it returns to your space instead.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Urn of Ashes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3420)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Void" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=510" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 307

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This pewter urn contains the ashes of a benevolent ancestor, with a sliver of lingering spirit that strives to protect you.

**Activate—Doom the Urn** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561))  **Trigger** You would become [doomed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=67), or your doomed value would increase; **Requirements** The ashes aren't doomed;; **Effect** The ashes in the urn intervene, taking the doomed condition in your place, and you don't gain or increase the value of your doomed condition. Each night when you get a full night's rest, you can reduce your own doomed condition or that of the urn, but not both.

**Activate—Spirit's Wrath** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([attack](/Traits.aspx?ID=540), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** The urn shoots a bolt of void energy at a foe within 30 feet. Attempt a spell attack roll against the target's AC, using a modifier of +15 or your own spell attack modifier, whichever is higher. On a success, the bolt deals 4d4 void damage (doubled on a critical success).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Urn of Ashes

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- This bizarre staff is made from oak, capped with a cluster of eye-shaped gemstones that seem to move and undulate at the corner of your vision. While wielding the staff, you can peer through the eyes on the staff rather than your own, using your normal visual senses (including any benefits of spells like [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)). You can maneuver the staff to see things around corners, at higher elevations, or in places where the staff can fit but your head can't. This doesn't provide sufficient line of effect to target creatures around corners. The eyes are as vulnerable as your eyes and can be affected by anything that alters your vision, such as a blinding flash of light. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. Staff of Impossible Visions **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 263 **Price** 230 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482)
  • **1st** [_fear_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1524), [_phantom pain_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1632)
  • **2nd** [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [_paranoia_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1623)
Staff of Impossible Visions (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 263 **Price** 1,800 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **3rd** [_hypnotize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564), [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622)
  • **4th** [_countless eyes_](/Spells.aspx?ID=680), [_vision of death_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1742)
  • **5th** [_strange geometry_](/Spells.aspx?ID=719)
Staff of Impossible Visions (Major) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 263 **Price** 10,000 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **6th** [_blinding fury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=672), [_phantasmal calamity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630)
  • **7th** [_visions of danger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=358), [_warp mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1754)
Staff of Impossible Visions (True) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 263 **Price** 70,000 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **8th** [_spirit song_](/Spells.aspx?ID=303), [_uncontrollable dance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1730)
  • **9th** [_unfathomable song_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1731), [_phantasmagoria_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1629)
name: Staff of Impossible Visions

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A large, stylized symbol of an eye adorns the top of this wooden staff, representing the watchful eye of the divine powers. The bearer of the staff can guide and protect, seeing bounties and tragedies that could befall them in the future. When wielding this staff, you gain a +1 item bonus to Survival checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) or [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) and to Religion checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. Staff of Providence **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 230 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549)
  • **1st** [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [_create water_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1476)
  • **2nd** [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [_create food_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1475), [_see the unseen_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663), [_status_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1690)
Staff of Providence (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 900 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **3rd** [_safe passage_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1659), [_wanderer's guide_](/Spells.aspx?ID=368)
  • **4th** [_cleanse affliction_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1467), [_dispelling globe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1494), _status_
Staff of Providence (Major) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 4,100 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **5th** _dispelling globe_, [_scouting eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661), _see the unseen_
  • **6th** _dispelling globe_, [_scintillating safeguard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=713), [_truesight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1727)
Staff of Providence (True) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308, [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 264 **Price** 64,000 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **7th** _dispelling globe_, [_energy aegis_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1512)
  • **8th** _dispelling globe_, [_divine inspiration_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1497)
  • **9th** _dispelling globe_, [_foresight_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1537), [_overwhelming presence_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1621)
name: Staff of Providence

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A large, stylized symbol of an eye adorns the top of this wooden staff, representing the watchful eye of the divine powers. The bearer of the staff can guide and protect, seeing bounties and tragedies that could befall them in the future. When wielding this staff, you gain a +1 item bonus to Survival checks to [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406) or [Subsist](/Actions.aspx?ID=2368) and to Religion checks to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A staff of the tempest is usually crafted from the wood of a tree struck by lightning. It's often gnarled and blackened with the occasional spark of electricity flashing from its length. While wielding the staff, your vision is less inhibited by stormy weather. While you hold the staff, you ignore the [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62) condition from mist, precipitation, and the like. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed ranks of all listed spells. Staff of the Tempest **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308 **Price** 230 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **Cantrip** [_electric arc_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1509)
  • **1st** [_hydraulic push_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1561), [_thunderstrike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1721)
  • **2nd** [_mist_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1606), [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1651) (electricity only), _thunderstrike_
Staff of the Tempest (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308 **Price** 900 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **3rd** [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586), [_wall of wind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1753)
  • **4th** [_hydraulic torrent_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1562), _lightning bolt_
Staff of the Tempest (Major) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 308 **Price** 4,000 gp **Bulk** 1 ---
  • **5th** _hydraulic torrent_, [_lightning storm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=701)
  • **6th** [_chain lightning_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1462), _hydraulic torrent_
name: Staff of the Tempest

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wand of Crackling Lightning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3424)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=586" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=731" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 309

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wand is made of two copper plates and a ceramic center. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586), but the spell's area is twice as wide (two adjacent and parallel 120-foot lines) and creatures that fail their save are [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 round.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_lightning bolt_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1586) of the appropriate rank.

This wand is a length of wrought black iron.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480). Each creature that ends its turn within the spell's area must succeed at a DC 20 Will save or become [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 1 (frightened 2 on a critical failure).

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_darkness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1480) of the appropriate rank.

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
<trait label="Vitality" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=509" />
<trait label="Wand" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=731" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 309

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This alabaster wand has a clear crystal at the tip.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) at the indicated rank. After you cast the spell, at the start of your next turn, excess healing magic wells up from the wand and heals you, as though you cast the 1-action version of _heal_ on yourself at the same spell rank. You gain this benefit only once per turn, even if you cast multiple _heal_ spells from wands of _overflowing life_ in the same turn.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1554) of the appropriate rank.

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The length of this wand is a pair of twisted giant spider legs.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> Cast a Spell;  **Frequency** once per day, plus overcharge;   **Effect** You cast [_web_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2045), but the strands of webbing are toxic. Any creature that fails its Athletics check or Reflex save to navigate the web takes 1d6 poison damage.

**Craft Requirements** Supply a casting of [_web_](/Spells.aspx?ID=374) of the appropriate rank.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"
right="Item 7"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 309

**Price** 360 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wand of the Spider (4th-Rank Spell)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 309

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L

</row>

</column>

---

The strands deal 2d6 poison damage plus 1d6 persistent poison damage.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Alchemist Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3431)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 605

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These brass goggles are engraved with flame patterns and have thick, heavy lenses. While worn, they give you a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) alchemical items. When making [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with alchemical bombs, you ignore lesser [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) . If your Strike with an alchemical bomb fails (but doesn't critically fail), you gain a +1 item bonus to the splash damage the target of the Strike takes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 605

**Price** 100 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Alchemist Goggles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 605

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus increases to +2, and the item bonus to splash damage on a miss is +2.

level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Alchemist Goggles (Major)

    pfs="Standard"

[Alchemist Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3431)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 605

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These brass goggles are engraved with flame patterns and have thick, heavy lenses. While worn, they give you a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) alchemical items. When making [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with alchemical bombs, you ignore lesser [cover](/Rules.aspx?ID=2372) . If your Strike with an alchemical bomb fails (but doesn't critically fail), you gain a +1 item bonus to the splash damage the target of the Strike takes.

markdown:
**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310 **Usage** worn cloak **Bulk** 1 --- This bearskin includes the head and bared teeth of the mighty creature from which it was taken. When worn, the cloak drapes over your head and around your shoulders, imbuing you with a bear's ferocity. If you have the [Rage](/Actions.aspx?ID=2802) action, while raging, you grow jaws that deal 1d10 piercing damage and claws that deal 1d6 slashing damage and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=526) trait. This transformation is a morph effect, and both the jaws and claws are unarmed attacks in the brawling weapon group. You gain the benefits of a +1 _[weapon potency rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830)_ and a _[striking rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829)_ with these attacks (gaining a +1 item bonus to attack rolls and increasing the number of weapon damage dice by one).
If you have an [animal instinct](/Instincts.aspx?ID=1) and the bestial rage instinct ability, instead of gaining these unarmed attacks, your unarmed attacks from the bestial rage instinct ability gain the benefits of a _+2 [weapon potency rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830)_ and a _[greater striking rune](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) _ (granting a +2 item bonus to attack rolls and increasing the number of weapon damage dice by two). Berserker's Cloak **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 606 **Price** 2,000 gp **Bulk** 1 Berserker's Cloak (Greater) **Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 606 **Price** 40,000 gp **Bulk** 1 --- You gain the benefits of a _+2 weapon potency_ rune and a _greater striking rune_, or a _+3 weapon potency_ rune and a _major striking_ rune if you have an animal instinct and the bestial rage instinct ability.
name: Berserker's Cloak

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fire-Jump Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3433)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=604" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

**Price** 940 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This black, metal ring is inset with rubies that occasionally give off wisps of smoke. It grants you a +2 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks.

**Activate—Fire Jump** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Actions.aspx?ID=2229), [teleportation](/Traits.aspx?ID=710)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You Stride (or Burrow or Fly, if you have the corresponding Speed) into any fire large enough to contain you, including magical fires. You vanish into the fire and take no damage from it. You can sense all sufficiently large fires within 100 feet of where you vanish, and you reemerge from any of those fires, either within the fire or adjacent to it. If you end your movement in the fire, it affects you as normal.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Fire-Jump Ring

This ice-blue half-mask is adorned with a wicked silver grin that covers only the wearer's mouth. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks.

**Activate—Hideous Wail** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The mask emits a soul-chilling scream that deals 6d10 void damage to each living creature in a 20-foot emanation (DC 25 basic Fortitude save).

>
[Helm of Zeal](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3435)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This elaborate helmet is emblazoned with the divine symbols of a deity chosen when the helmet was crafted. You gain a +2 item bonus to that deity's Divine Skill.

**Activate—Rally to the cause** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a devotion spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Activate—Divine Fervor** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You've just used your champion's reaction;  **Effect** You gain an additional reaction you can use only for your champion's reaction. You lose this reaction if you don't use it by the start of your next turn. 

**Craft Requirements** You're a champion of the deity represented by the helmet.

>
[Prognostic Veil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3436)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=579" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gauzy purple veil is embroidered with symbols of divinatory significance. As your curse worsens, the veil ripples in an ever-increasing unseen wind. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks.

**Activate—Remember the Future** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a revelation spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Activate—Twist the Skeins of Fate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** oncer per day; **Trigger** An ally within 30 feet is about to attempt a saving throw; **Requirements** You have the [cursebound](/Traits.aspx?ID=320) condition; **Effect** The ally gains a status bonus to the saving throw equal to the value of your cursebound condition. 

**Craft Requirements** You're an oracle.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ring of Maniacal Devices](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3437)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magic ring seems like simple tarnished brass, but it enhances your curiosity about traps and devices of all kinds. You can use an Interact action to pull a [thieves' toolkit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758) from the ring. This toolkit appears in your hand and any part of it folds back into the ring if they would leave your possession. They grant you a +2 item bonus to Thievery checks to [Disable a Device](/Actions.aspx?ID=2411) and to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412), and the ring's insights grant you the same bonus to Crafting checks to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385) and [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=2384) snares and traps.

**Activate—Fireball Trap** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You create the effects of a [_rune trap_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=124) ritual containing [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530). You can have only one trapped rune from a ring of maniacal devices active at a time, even if you have multiple rings, and the rune disappears if you lose your investiture in the ring.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 11"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ring of Maniacal Devices
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 614

**Price** 1,175 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 18"
pfs="Standard"
>
Ring of Maniacal Devices (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Player Core 2](/Sources.aspx?ID=227) pg. 310, [Core Rulebook](/Sources.aspx?ID=1) pg. 614

**Price** 21,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The ring grants a +3 bonus. Activating it creates a rune trap containing your choice of either a 7th-rank [_howling blizzard_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1559) or a 7th-rank [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1530).
name: Ring of Maniacal Devices

This clear crystal pendant contains a drop of blood from a sorcerer that expands and contracts as you cast spells. A _sanguine pendant_ is associated with a specific sorcerer bloodline, and only sorcerers with that bloodline can invest this item. This item gains the trait matching the tradition of that bloodline. The pendant grants a +2 item bonus to both of your bloodline skills.

**Activate—Blood's Call** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a bloodline spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Craft Requirements** You're a sorcerer with the associated bloodline.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=609" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This humble sash can be worn around the waist or across the chest. A sash of prowess often bears a coloration or a pattern that represents the monastery in which you trained but can also sport religious symbology, such as the open hand of Irori. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) skill checks.

**Activate—Effortless Mastery** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You succeed at an Acrobatics or Athletics skill check; **Effect** You critically succeed instead.

**Activate—Reserves of Inner Strength** <actions string="Free Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a qi spell. If you don't spend this Focus Point by the end of this turn, it's lost.

**Craft Requirements** You're a monk who can cast qi spells.

markdown:
May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 181 **Price** 1,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- Originally constructed by the Thassilonian wizard Liralarue to serve as a key for a treasure vault, this clockwork songbird has other functions as well. Liralarue took the songbird apart once she realized Thassilon faced a mysterious doom, then hid the parts in her domain. The parts survived but never traveled too far from the region that would become known as the Sandpoint Hinterlands.
Before the _clockwork songbird_ can be used, its six components (the body, the left and right wings a one piece, the legs and tail, the head, and the winding key) must first be recovered. Once all six parts are at hand, a character can reassemble the _clockwork songbird_ as a 1-hour activity that requires a DC 27 Crafting check. A character who critically fails at this check causes the components to generate a blast of devastating sound, inflicting 7d6 sonic damage to the character (DC 27 basic Reflex save).
Once reconstructed, the clockwork songbird can be activated in the following three ways. **Activate** Interact; **Effect** The _clockwork songbird_ animates and sings a complex birdsong that lasts for 1 minute. The song is soothing and pleasant but impossible to accurately repeat, as each time the clockwork is activated in this way, its next song differs slightly. Regardless, this activation also functions to activate the portal in area **H13** in the Pit. **Activate** Interact (10 minutes); **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The songbird leaps into the air and flies in a circle, swooping around an area around you for 10 minutes as it sings. The songbird never travels further than 50 feet from the location where you activated it. It returns to that location at the end of the duration, at which point the songbird casts [_private sanctum_](/Spells.aspx?ID=235) at that location before going quiet once again. The effect remains in place for 24 hours. **Activate** Interact; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You place the _clockwork songbird_ on a stable surface, and it casts an [_alarm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1439) spell heightened to 3rd level to your specifications. When the _alarm_ is triggered, it does so in the form of a loud warbling birdsong sung by the clockwork. If the clockwork songbird is moved from its location before the _alarm_ spell's duration has run its course, the spell ends.
name: Clockwork Songbird

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 181

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The fabric of a _cultist cowl_ is either raspy burlap, durable cotton, or fine silk, depending on the item's power (and thus the implied import of the worshipper who wears it). When a character who worships a single deity dons a cultist cowl, the cowl's colors change to match those sacred to that deity, and the deity's symbol or rune appears on the lower front of the cowl that hangs just over the chest. These colors and symbols remain until a different worshipper wears the cowl. While worn, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks. You also gain a +1 item bonus to all skill checks attempted to aid a ritual by being a secondary caster.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Cultist Cowl](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3443)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 181

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The fabric of a _cultist cowl_ is either raspy burlap, durable cotton, or fine silk, depending on the item's power (and thus the implied import of the worshipper who wears it). When a character who worships a single deity dons a cultist cowl, the cowl's colors change to match those sacred to that deity, and the deity's symbol or rune appears on the lower front of the cowl that hangs just over the chest. These colors and symbols remain until a different worshipper wears the cowl. While worn, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks. You also gain a +1 item bonus to all skill checks attempted to aid a ritual by being a secondary caster.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

level="2"

>
Cultist Cowl (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 181

**Price** 650 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The cowl grants a +2 item bonus, and this bonus also applies to checks attempted as a primary caster to perform a ritual. The cowl can also be activated. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The cowl casts [_crisis of faith_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478) (DC 25 Will) to your specification.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Limited"
>
Cultist Cowl (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 181

**Price** 14,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The cowl grants a +3 item bonus, and this bonus also applies to checks attempted as a primary caster to perform a ritual. The cowl can also be activated. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, command;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The cowl casts [_crisis of faith_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1478) (DC 37 Will) heightened to 7th-level to your specification.
name: Cultist Cowl

    level="1"
    right="Item 5"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Demortification Oil](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3444)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Oil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=121" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 182

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This foul-smelling oil has the appearance and odor of the greasy, thick fluids that seep from a decaying body. When you spread this oil on an intact corpse that has been dead no longer than 1 week, its decay fades and the corpse is restored to the condition it was just after death. _Demortification oil_ can't undo damage done to a corpse after death, such as consumption by scavengers. Applied to a non-incorporeal undead, a dose of _demortification oil_ grants the undead creature a +1 item bonus to Armor Class for 1 hour.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Demortification Oil

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Devil's Breath Incense](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3445)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 182

**Price** 70 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This 6-inch-long stick of gray-green incense has an unsettling, oily look to it. When burned, a stick of _devil's breath incense_ emits a brimstone-like stink and burns for 10 minutes. Varisian tales claim the stink of this incense closely matches the odor of the breath of the [Sandpoint Devil](/Monsters.aspx?ID=791), although it doesn't come close to the full stink of its flaming exhalations. A creature within 10 feet of a stick of burning _devil's breath incense_ must succeed at a DC 23 Fortitude save or become [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure), after which they're temporarily immune to the incense for 1 hour.

 While a stick of _devil's breath incense_ is burning, an adjacent creature can lean over the stick to inhale forcefully as an Interact action. Doing so consumes the entire stick immediately and automatically makes the creature sickened 3, but when the creature exhales its next breath, the cloud of smoke they breathe out attempts to carry with it a single curse the creature is afflicted by. The incense has a [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) level of 4 and a +12 modifier for the roll. If used to remove the effects of the Sandpoint Devil's Accursed Breath (or similar effects, such as the Cursed Flames that the goblin witch Minargul can create), the _devil's breath incense_ automatically succeeds at removing the curse, regardless of the level of the curse itself.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Devil's Breath Incense
