markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs=""
>
[Dream Lens](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3446)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Artifact" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=253" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 182

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 10
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _Dream Lens_ is located far from Sandpoint, housed in an obelisk of pink-veined marble in the lost city of Ilvarandin deep down in the Vaults of Orv. Here, this sinister artifact can capture the dreaming mind of a mortal anywhere in the world who has fallen under the influence of midnight milk. The Dream Lens is a large convex crystal housed in a series of metal armatures, itself guarded by powerful constructs and arcane countermeasures. 

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> envision, Interact;    **Requirements** You’re an [intellect devourer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=703) whose mind has been linked to a user of midnight milk, and that creature is still dreaming in your mind; **Effect** The _Dream Lens_ establishes a link between you and the dreaming host, allowing you to rip and tear into the host's mind from afar. You can attempt two talon Strikes against the dreaming creature, using the same attack modifier as your highest attack modifier. These strikes gain the [death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571) and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits, and damage caused by them is mental damage, not slashing damage. If this kills the target, the intellect devourer can use Body Thief against the target as a free action, teleporting from the _Dream Lens_ directly into the skull of its new, freshly slain host. If these strikes don't kill the target, it can immediately attempt a new Fortitude save against the DC of the midnight milk it's currently under the effects of. If it succeeds at this saving throw, the target immediately awakes (retaining only vague memories of an awful nightmare) and is no longer affected by that dose of midnight milk.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Dream Lens

    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Flickering](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3447)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 182

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _flickering_ rune causes a weapon to shimmer, grow blurry and indistinct, and momentarily turn invisible at random intervals for a second or two. A _flickering_ weapon adds its item bonus from its potency rune to the DC against attempts to [Disarm](/Actions.aspx?ID=2383) or [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=2410) it. On a critical hit, the weapon flashes bright pulses of color into the creature's eyes, [dazzling](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) the creature for 1 round (this effect has the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) trait). 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The flickering weapon casts [_blur_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1455) to your specification.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Flickering

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 7"
    pfs=""
>
[Ghost Stone](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3448)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 183

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 18
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The _Ghost Stone_ is a stationary magic item, a 10-foot-long spindle-shaped crystal floating and slowly rotating within a hemispherical chamber in the Pit (area **E10**). If moved from this chamber, the _Ghost Stone_ is destroyed. It can also be destroyed via damage (AC 28, Hardness 16, HP 60). If it's destroyed, the trapped Varisian spirits within are finally released; as a vortex of vaguely humanoid shapes swirls around the room, all PCs in area **E10** are filled with sensations of elation and freedom. The departing ghosts grant each PC the first activation result below, but after that, the _Ghost Stone_ crashes to the ground and shatters into mundane crystal, forever destroyed. 

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> (command, envision, Interact);     **Effect** The _Ghost Stone_ grants a terrifying vision of doom. For the PCs, this vision is of seven apocalyptic dooms that descend upon Sandpoint.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> 10 minutes (command, envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _Ghost Stone_ casts [_read omens_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647) to your specifications.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Stone

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs=""
>
[The Hollow Star](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3449)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Unique" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=161" />
<trait label="Chaotic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=25" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 183

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This roughly spherical mass of orange crystal is about the size of a human head. The sphere is opaque but glows from within as if it contained a bright flickering flame—the _Hollow Star_ emits bright light in a 60-foot radius (and dim light to the next 60 feet). Any creature within the bright light of the _Hollow Star_ gains a +1 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) checks. [Deros](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=388) in the bright light shed by the _Hollow Star_ suffer distracting but pleasant hallucinations and strange visions, and they become [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 when in this illumination. If you're lawful, you're [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=71) 2 while carrying the _Hollow Star_. If you touch the _Hollow Star_, your dreams the next time you sleep are strange, unsettling, hard to remember, and carry a vague sense of elation, doom, or both. Upon awakening, you must succeed at a DC 25 Will save or become stupefied 1 by unsettling dreams that feel disturbingly like memories from a life lived on a distant dying planet.

 The Keepers of the _Hollow Star_ believe that this unique magical item, if allowed to bathe for an unspecified amount of time in sunlight, will bring [Azathoth](/Deities.aspx?ID=161) into that world's sun to destroy it. However, the item has no actual capacity to do so. Yet. 

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ([divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47))  **Trigger** You cast a spell or use an effect that has the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) trait;  **Effect** You speak in Aklo when using the linguistic spell or effect, even if you don't normally speak Aklo. If the spell or effect requires a spell attack roll, you gain a +1 item bonus to the roll. If the spell or effect can be resisted with a saving throw, all saves against the effect suffer a –1 item penalty.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([death](/Traits.aspx?ID=571), [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _Hollow Star_ casts [_phantasmal killer_](/Spells.aspx?ID=219) (DC 25). The target of this effect receives a brief but overwhelming vision of the world ending as an immense explosion in the sky expands to engulf the world.

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([conjuration](/Traits.aspx?ID=33)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _Hollow Star_ conjures an aberration to fight for you. This works like [_summon animal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1694), except you summon a common creature that has the [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=523) trait and whose level is 3 or lower.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: The Hollow Star

    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Liar's Lexicon](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3450)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Grimoire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=387" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 184

**Price** 1,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The covers of this book are bound in scratchy, purple leather—hide harvested from a mind-manipulating wormlike monster known as a [seugathi](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=212). The margins of this grimoire contain hundreds of tips and notes to avoid having your lies discovered. When you prepare spells from a _liar's lexicon_, you gain a +2 item bonus to Deception checks to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389).

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([metamagic](/Traits.aspx?ID=107)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** If your next action is to cast a spell that has the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590) trait, your skill at manipulation and exploiting emotion enhance the spell. The spell gains the [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=642) trait. Attempt a Deception check to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=2389) as you cast the spell against the Perception DC of all those observing you. Those you succeed against think you were merely talking, not casting a spell on a creature.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Liar's Lexicon

</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=6" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Drug" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=261" />
<trait label="Ingested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=95" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 184

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Midnight milk is a strange and powerful drug, originally invented by the intellect devourers of the alien city of Ilvarandin to remotely steal the bodies of distant dreaming victims through the use of an artifact called the _[Dream Lens](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3446)_. Pure midnight milk is incredibly rare—almost all of that found beyond the vault of Ilvarandin is instead cut to reduce costs and expenses. Crafting a dose of this dangerous drug requires a wide range of reagents; the most difficult to acquire of these reagents are vials of refined “sweat” harvested via a mithral blade from the fleshy fronds of a rare form of cavetongue fungus known as authul, which grows wild only in remote corners of the Vaults of Orv. When an alchemist mixes midnight milk, they must do so while in a trancelike state that approximates the dreaming mind—a classic method of reaching this state involves the repetition of a wordless chant spoken in a specific meter and rhyme scheme (one that the poet Vumeshki unknowingly duplicated with his dream-inspired poem, “Ilvarandin”). Recently, an experimental form of the drug created by the alchemist Aliver Podiker has been developed, but so far, attempts to replicate refined midnight milk using these methods have met with failure.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

---

</column>

---

Pure midnight milk is incredibly rare—almost all of that found beyond the vault of Ilvarandin is instead cut to reduce costs and expenses. Crafting a dose of this dangerous drug requires a wide range of reagents; the most difficult to acquire of these reagents are vials of refined “sweat” harvested via a mithral blade from the fleshy fronds of a rare form of cavetongue fungus known as authul, which grows wild only in remote corners of the Vaults of Orv. When an alchemist mixes midnight milk, they must do so while in a trancelike state that approximates the dreaming mind—a classic method of reaching this state involves the repetition of a wordless chant spoken in a specific meter and rhyme scheme (one that the poet Vumeshki unknowingly duplicated with his dream-inspired poem, “Ilvarandin”). Recently, an experimental form of the drug created by the alchemist Aliver Podiker has been developed, but so far, attempts to replicate refined midnight milk using these methods have met with failure.

</column>

May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint **Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 184 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L --- _Monarch_ is an ancient starknife, once wielded by a priest of the Desnan sect known as the Order of the Starless Night, an organization devoted to protecting Golarion from the horrors of the Dark Tapestry. The weapon is made of [low-grade silver](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2920), and its blades gleam with soft light, equivalent to that of a [candle](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2709) (the glow can be extinguished or activated by the weapon's carrier by activating Monarch as an envision action). The blades bear etchings of monarch butterflies in flight.
When discovered in the Pit, _Monarch_ functions as a _[+1 striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2861) [returning](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2845) [starknife](/Weapons.aspx?ID=399)_ that can't have additional property runes, as it's a specific item. _Monarch_ deals an additional 1d6 damage to creatures with the [aberration](/Traits.aspx?ID=523) trait, provided the creature isn't disguised as another creature. **Activate** 10 minutes (envision, Interact); **Frequency** once per day; **Requirements** _Monarch_ must possess a major gift; **Effect** _Monarch_ casts [_dream message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1503) (heightened to 4th level) to your specifications. ---
**Minor Gifts**: [wind barrier](/Relics.aspx?ID=3), [word of faith](/Relics.aspx?ID=15)
**Major Gifts**: [propelling winds](/Relics.aspx?ID=5), [righteous call](/Relics.aspx?ID=17)
**Grand Gift**: [azata's grace](/Relics.aspx?ID=196)
name: Monarch

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Nightmare](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3453)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 185

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** etched onto a weapon

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _nightmare_ rune creates minor phantasmal alterations to a weapon's appearance so that those who look upon it see subtle reminders of their deepest fears. An arachnophobe might interpret the cross guard of a _nightmare_ longsword to look like curving, twitching spider legs, for example, while someone who's afraid of sickness might see a _nightmare_ club as a diseased length of bone crawling with flies. These images are all in the mind of the observer, but they also infuse the weapon with additional power. When you hit with a nightmare weapon, add 1d6 mental damage to the damage dealt. In addition, on a critical hit, the target becomes [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=94) 1 by overwhelming visions in their mind of personal horrors that linger. If you critically hit a creature that's already stupefied, it becomes [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) 2 as well. These critical hit effects have the [emotion](/Traits.aspx?ID=590), [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598), and [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647) traits. 

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The _nightmare weapon_ casts [_nightmare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1615) to your specifications.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Nightmare

    pfs="Limited"
>
[Philter of Empty Dreams](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3454)
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Seven Dooms for Sandpoint</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=36" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Poison" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=126" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #200: Seven Dooms for Sandpoint](/Sources.aspx?ID=229) pg. 185

**Price** 90 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>Interact</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _philter of empty dreams_ is a dose of glowing blue liquid that prevents dreams. When you drink a _philter of empty dreams_, you have no dreams for the next 8 hours and gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against dream- or nightmare-themed effects (including saving throws against the effects of [midnight milk](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3451)). If you're suffering from an addiction to midnight milk when you drink a _philter of empty dreams_, the potion attempts to counteract the addiction with a counteract level of 4 and a +15 modifier for the roll. If an [intellect devourer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=703) who's controlling a stolen body uses the body to drink a _philter of empty dreams_, the intellect devourer must succeed at a DC 24 Fortitude save or be forced to Exit the Body immediately.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Philter of Empty Dreams

---

This strong, clear liquor is made from fermented rice. When you drink a cup of iron wine, your sweat becomes highly combustive for the next 10 minutes, igniting with the slightest bit of friction. This causes your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=719) attacks to deal an additional 1d4 [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=604) damage for the duration of the effect. Drinking more than one cup of iron wine in a single day gives you [weakness](/Rules.aspx?ID=2357)  5 to fire until your next daily preparations.

Black swill with multicolored ribbons makes this vinegar look like an oil spill. Ingesting a dose of rainbow vinegar makes your sweat acidic and nonconductive for 10 minutes. [Vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=480) find this vinegar particularly harmful and take an additional 2d4 acid damage instead. Taking more than one dose of rainbow vinegar in a single day gives you [ weakness ](/Rules.aspx?ID=2317)5 to acid until your next daily preparations.

<trait label="Consumable" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=564" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 105

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** L
</row>

**Access** Tian Xia Origin

<row gap="medium">
<row gap="tiny"><row>**Activate**</row><actions string="Single Action" /><row>([manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104))</row></row>

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though these preserved vegetables aren't actual moonflowers, they're made using a moonflower-distilled vinegar. When consumed, tendrils sprout from your veins and curl around you, granting you a +2 status bonus to saves against [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) effects for 10 minutes and reducing your [wounded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=99) condition by up to 2. Eating more than one preserved moonflower dish in a day doesn't grant further benefits and makes you [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 1.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Preserved Moonflower

</row>

---

White, pillowy dough surrounds an interior containing minced meats, spices, and herbs. The dough is steamed with elemental magic to make it light and airy while keeping the meaty center moist. When you consume a bun, a set of small clouds form around your feet that grant you a [_fly_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1534) Speed of 30 feet or your speed, whichever is lower, for 1 round.

</column>

</title>

</row>

---

This robust porridge—made from a cockatrice distilled into a broth, hot peppers from a demonic source, and various toppings—causes diners to utter a ghostly wail from the intense heat. Consuming the porridge grants you a +2 item bonus to saving throws against being [petrified](/Conditions.aspx?ID=87) for 1 hour. You also gain [resistance ](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 3 against physical damage for the duration.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ghost Fowl Porridge

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Caress of the Great Serpent](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3461)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=721" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 126

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 2
</row>

<row gap="medium">
**Base Weapon** [Urumi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=306)
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This +[3](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [extending](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2836) [urumi](/Weapons.aspx?ID=306) was fashioned after the legendary eightheaded orochi serpent, with its whiplike metal blades carved to resemble the many heads of the mythical beast, and its hilt wrapped in scaled leather. These weapons are commonly used by those who worship an orochi, raiding and threatening settlements to obtain sacrifices for the beast in hopes of being granted some modicum of its power. 

**Activate—Serpent’s Kiss** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You use your urumi as an effigy with which to call upon an [orochi](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1416)’s power, offering your blood in exchange. You can make up to eight [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with a –2 penalty, each against a different target within 30 feet. For each Strike you choose to make, you take 1d6 piercing damage. Each attack counts toward your multiple attack penalty, but don’t increase your penalty until you’ve made all your attacks.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Caress of the Great Serpent

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 128, [Book of the Dead](/Sources.aspx?ID=118) pg. 18 **Price** 15,000 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398) --- From blade to pommel, this sword is carved from a branch of the now-extinct celestial peach tree. The blade of this +[3](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [greater striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [holy](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2842) [vitalizing](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2852) [peachwood](/Equipment.aspx?ID=1541) [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=398) has ancient runes that can permanently destroy the most powerful undead—if you are willing to pay the price.
An [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=722) creature of 12th level or lower that takes damage from a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) with the sword takes 10d6 [vitality](/Traits.aspx?ID=509) damage with a DC 35 basic Fortitude save. This damage is separate from the Strike itself and isn't included in any effect based on the Strike's damage. **Activate—Violent Exorcism** ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; Trigger You critically hit an undead creature with the sword; **Effect** The creature must succeed at a DC 35 Fortitude save or be [destroyed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=46). If the undead fails its saving throw and is destroyed, you suffer a backlash, taking 1d6 [void](/Traits.aspx?ID=510) damage per level of the destroyed undead.
name: Celestial Peachwood Sword

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 19"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Cloak of Waves & Clouds](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3463)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=637" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=644" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 128

**Price** 38,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This magical cloak was crafted from the feathers and scales of a legendary giant fish that could transform into a resplendent bird. 

**Activate—Cut Air and Sea** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645))    **Effect** The cloak ripples, becoming either giant eagle feathers or iridescent fish scales. Until you next [Activate](/Actions.aspx?ID=100) the cloak, you gain either a [swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381) Speed of 60 feet and the ability to breathe underwater or a [fly](/Actions.aspx?ID=2312) Speed of 40 feet.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Cloak of Waves & Clouds

**Source** [Tian Xia Character Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=230) pg. 128 **Price** 500 gp **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** L **Base Weapon** [Kris](/Weapons.aspx?ID=264) --- A hauntingly beautiful and masterfully crafted blade, this +[1](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2830) [striking](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2829) [wounding](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2854) [kris](/Weapons.aspx?ID=264) has been whet with the spilled blood of its creator, imbuing violent intent within its crimson curves. When you critically succeed at a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) made with a deathseeker, the target feels the blade's unbridled bloodlust trying to consume it and must attempt a DC 24 Will save; this effect has the [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631) trait. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune for 24 hours
**Success** The target takes an additional 1 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29), and the DC for recovering from persistent bleed damage is 17, or 12 with particularly effective assistance.
**Failure** As success, except the target is also [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round. It gets a flat check to recover from this confusion when it critically succeeds at a Strike against another creature or reduces another living being to 0 Hit Points, but not when it takes damage.
**Critical Failure** As success, except the target is also confused for 3 rounds. It gets a flat check to recover from this confusion when it critically succeeds at a Strike against another creature or reduces another living being to 0 Hit Points, but not when it takes damage.
name: Deathseeker
